== Table Description ==
A dataset linking method parameters to their metadata in a codebase, including unique identifiers for entities, parameter types, and parameter names across millions of records.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] Id: Unique identifier for each comment entity, with examples such as "2762802", "882395", "3275163". Contains 1,000,000 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "2762802", "882395", "3275163", "3386063", "2566273".
[EntityId] MethodId: Unique identifier for the method to which the parameter belongs, with examples such as "1315550", "1787162", "706808". Contains 522,684 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "1315550", "1787162", "706808", "1085484", "1320943".
[MediaMetadata] Type: Data type of the method parameter, including types like "System.String", "dynamic", and "TfsWorkbench.Core.Interfaces.IWorkbenchItem". Media metadata including 62,263 unique attributes such as "System.String", "dynamic", "TfsWorkbench.Core.Interfaces.IWorkbenchItem", "Pondman.MediaPortal.MediaBrowser.GUI.GUICommon.Window<TParameters>(Pondman.MediaPortal.MediaBrowser....", "Kalibrasi.Data.MDepartementFieldIndex", with common themes including N/A
[EntityId] Name: Name of the method parameter, with examples such as "ascxControlToLoad", "_vgpu_type", "factory". Contains 84,999 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "ascxControlToLoad", "_vgpu_type", "factory", "platformName", "maxCreateIndexDurationInMinutes".

== Table Description ==
Sales and order data for the East region of a superstore, including customer, product, and financial metrics across five years.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] Row ID: Unique row identifier, e.g., "4863", "6321", "8702" Contains 5,696 unique identifiers with an average length of 4.1 characters (e.g., "4863", "6321", "8702", "8694", "6208").
[Identifier] Order ID: Order identifier with region and year prefix, e.g., "US-2012-115343", "CA-2011-140004", "US-2012-160857" Contains 2,802 unique identifiers with an average length of 14.0 characters (e.g., "US-2012-115343", "CA-2011-140004", "US-2012-160857", "CA-2012-114811", "US-2014-118087").
[EventTimestamp] Order Date: Date when the order was placed, ranging from 2013-01-05 to 2018-12-30 Event timestamps ranging from 2013-01-05T00:00 to 2018-12-30T00:00 across 5,696 recorded occurrences.
[EventTimestamp] Ship Date: Date when the order was shipped, ranging from 2013-01-12 to 2019-01-04 Event timestamps ranging from 2013-01-12T00:00 to 2019-01-04T00:00 across 5,696 recorded occurrences.
[Category] Ship Mode: Shipping method used, including "Standard Class", "Second Class", "First Class", "Same Day" Contains 4 unique categories, including Standard Class, Second Class, First Class, Same Day
[Identifier] Customer ID: Customer identifier, e.g., "MD-17350", "EB-13705", "LC-17140" Contains 674 unique identifiers with an average length of 8.0 characters (e.g., "MD-17350", "EB-13705", "LC-17140", "BB-11545", "MG-17650").
[AdministrativeRegion] Region: Region of operation, consistently "East" 1 administrative regions including "East", with names averaging 4.0 characters
[Identifier] Product ID: Product identifier with category and code, e.g., "FUR-TA-10003748", "OFF-BI-10001116", "TEC-PH-10003095" Contains 1,422 unique identifiers with an average length of 15.0 characters (e.g., "FUR-TA-10003748", "OFF-BI-10001116", "FUR-FU-10003268", "OFF-ST-10004634", "TEC-PH-10003095").
[FinancialMetric] Sales: Sales amount in USD, ranging from 0.85 to 11,199.97, average 238.34 Financial values ranging from 0.85 to 11,199.97 with an average of 238.34 across 5,696 entries.
[QuantityMetric] Quantity: Number of units sold, ranging from 1 to 14, average 3.73 Quantitative values ranging from 1 to 14 with an average of 3.73 across 5,696 entries.
[PercentageMetric] Discount: Discount applied as a decimal fraction, ranging from 0 to 0.70, average 0.15 Percentage values ranging from 0 to 0.70 with an average of 0.15
[FinancialMetric] Profit: Profit generated per order, ranging from -6,599.98 to 5,039.99, average 32.14 Financial values ranging from -6,599.98 to 5,039.99 with an average of 32.14 across 5,696 entries.
[AdministrativeRegion] Region: Redundant region field labeled "Region:12", consistently "East" with 100% nulls — likely a metadata artifact 1 administrative regions including "East", with names averaging 4.0 characters

== Table Description ==
A comprehensive dataset of European football match results spanning 2006 to 2021, including league divisions, home and away teams, full-time goals, and match outcomes.

== Column Information ==
[Category] Div: League division codes such as E1, E3, E2, SP2, I2 representing various European football leagues. Contains 21 unique categories, including E1, E3, E2, SP2, I2
[EventTimestamp] Date: Match dates ranging from 2005-07-29 to 2021-05-23, capturing over 123k matches across 16 years. Event timestamps ranging from 2005-07-29T00:00 to 2021-05-23T00:00 across 123,404 recorded occurrences.
[OrganizationUnit] HomeTeam: Home teams including Waregem, AZ Alkmaar, Livingston, Maidenhead, Reims. Contains 648 unique organizational units or teams, including "Waregem", "AZ Alkmaar", "Livingston", "Maidenhead", "Reims".
[OrganizationUnit] AwayTeam: Away teams including Man City, Notts County, Munich 1860, Scunthorpe, Crewe. Contains 648 unique organizational units or teams, including "Man City", "Notts County", "Munich 1860", "Scunthorpe", "Crewe".
[QuantityMetric] FTHG: Full-time home goals scored, ranging from 0 to 10, with an average of 1.48. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 10 with an average of 1.48 across 123,404 entries.
[QuantityMetric] FTAG: Full-time away goals scored, ranging from 0 to 13, with an average of 1.14. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 13 with an average of 1.14 across 123,404 entries.
[Category] FTR: Full-time result outcome: H (home win), A (away win), D (draw). Contains 3 unique categories, including H, A, D
[Year] season: Season years from 2006 to 2021, with a median season of 2013. Years ranging from 2,006 to 2,021 (median: 2013.00)

== Table Description ==
Table containing employee records for a sales team, with unique identifiers, names, and middle initials.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] EmployeeID: Unique identifier for each employee, such as 5, 19, or 9. Contains 22 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "5", "19", "9", "1", "23".
[PersonName] FirstName: First name of the employee, e.g., Sheryl, Meander, or Marjorie. Contains 22 unique names (e.g., "Sheryl", "Meander", "Marjorie", "Johnson", "Albert") with an average length of 6.1 characters.
[PersonName] MiddleInitial: Middle initial of the employee, e.g., o, t, or u. Contains 11 unique names (e.g., "o", "t", "u", "c", "r") with an average length of 1.0 characters.
[PersonName] LastName: Last name of the employee, e.g., Stringer, Locksley, or Dull. Contains 21 unique names (e.g., "Stringer", "Locksley", "Dull", "Panteley", "Straight") with an average length of 7.4 characters.

== Table Description ==
A dataset containing historical Olympic Games information, including unique identifiers, years, full names, and seasons (Summer/Winter) for 51 editions from 1896 to 2016.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] id: Unique numeric identifiers for Olympic editions, such as 37, 33, and 12. Contains 51 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "37", "33", "12", "10", "17".
[Year] games_year: Year the Olympic Games were held, ranging from 1896 to 2016, with median 1968. Years ranging from 1,896 to 2,016 (median: 1968)
[Title] games_name: Full name of the Olympic Games, such as '1956 Summer', '1928 Winter', and '1900 Summer'. Titles including 51 unique works such as "1956 Summer", "1928 Winter", "1900 Summer", "1956 Winter", "1912 Summer", with an average length of 11.0 characters.
[Category] season: Season of the Games, either 'Summer' or 'Winter'. Contains 2 unique categories, including Summer, Winter

== Table Description ==
A comprehensive dataset of food safety inspections conducted between 2010 and 2017, capturing inspection details, outcomes, inspector assignments, and licensing information across tens of thousands of food establishments.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] inspection_id: Unique identifier for each inspection record, such as 1734251, 1313208, 250649. Contains 143,870 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "1734251", "1313208", "250649", "1490999", "1559748".
[EventTimestamp] inspection_date: Date and time of the inspection, ranging from 2010-01-04T00:00 to 2017-02-17T00:00. Event timestamps ranging from 2010-01-04T00:00 to 2017-02-17T00:00 across 143,870 recorded occurrences.
[Category] inspection_type: Type of inspection performed, including Canvass, License, Canvass Re-Inspection, Complaint, License Re-Inspection. Contains 106 unique categories, including Canvass, License, Canvass Re-Inspection, Complaint, License Re-Inspection
[Category] results: Outcome of the inspection, such as Pass, Fail, Pass w/ Conditions, Out of Business, No Entry. Contains 7 unique categories, including Pass, Fail, Pass w/ Conditions, Out of Business, No Entry
[EntityId] employee_id: Identifier for the inspector who conducted the inspection, such as 141319, 143902, 138450. Contains 28 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "141319", "143902", "138450", "115790", "143902".
[EntityId] license_no: License number associated with the inspected establishment, such as 1932904, 3914, 2092903. Contains 31,733 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "1932904", "3914", "2092903", "1380994", "4428".
[EntityId] followup_to: Reference to a prior inspection ID that this inspection follows up on, such as 88434, 547657, 1138495; 81% nulls indicate most inspections are initial rather than follow-ups. Contains 26,594 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "88434", "547657", "1138495", "1966706", "1353761".

[TemporalDetail] ModifiedDate: Date and time the record was last modified, such as 2014-02-08 10:21.0, 2008-04-30 00:00.0. Temporal details including 2 unique values such as "2014-02-08 10:21.0", "2008-04-30 00:00.0", "2008-04-30 00:00.0", "2008-04-30 00:00.0", "2014-02-08 10:21.0"

== Table Description ==
A dataset of 40,325 user profiles from a developer community, capturing identity, activity metrics, profile details, and engagement statistics such as reputation, votes, views, and personal information.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] Id: Unique numeric identifiers for users, e.g., 37902, 44424, 6513. Contains 40,324 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "37902", "44424", "6513", "4681", "7184".
[FinancialMetric] Reputation: User reputation score, ranging from 1 to 87,393, with an average of 84.08. Financial values ranging from 1 to 87,393 with an average of 84.08 across 40,325 entries.
[EventTimestamp] CreationDate: Date and time when the user account was created, e.g., 2010-07-19T06:26, 2012-03-15T14:01. Event timestamps ranging from 2010-07-19T06:26 to 2014-09-14T01:44 across 40,325 recorded occurrences.
[PersonName] DisplayName: User-chosen display name, e.g., timday, deepML, Mike, user21680. Contains 35,635 unique names (e.g., "timday", "deepML", "user21680", "Mike", "user31378") with an average length of 8.5 characters.
[EventTimestamp] LastAccessDate: Date and time of the user’s last login or access, e.g., 2010-07-19T06:26, 2014-09-14T03:57. Event timestamps ranging from 2010-07-19T06:26 to 2014-09-14T03:57 across 40,325 recorded occurrences.
[DigitalResource] WebsiteUrl: Optional website URL provided by the user, e.g., http://example.com, github.com/user, with 80% nulls. Contains 7,787 unique digital resources across 8,121 entries, with primary domains including , , , ,
[GeographicDetail] Location: User’s geographic location, e.g., Sweden, Buenos Aires, Argentina, Austin, TX, Seattle, WA, with 71% nulls. Geographic details including 2,463 unique entries such as "Sweden", "Buenos Aires, Argentina", "Bangalore, India", "Austin, TX", "Seattle, WA", with an average length of 13.3 characters.
[Description] AboutMe: User-provided bio or about section, e.g., 'Student of computer science', 'Software developer passionate about data', with 77% nulls. Contains descriptive text with an average length of 117.2 characters (max: 2,879). Key terms include: student, data, software, university, developer.
[QuantityMetric] Views: Total number of times the user’s content was viewed, ranging from 0 to 20,932, average 8.91. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 20,932 with an average of 8.91 across 40,325 entries.
[QuantityMetric] UpVotes: Number of upvotes received on user contributions, ranging from 0 to 11,442, average 6.59. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 11,442 with an average of 6.59 across 40,325 entries.
[QuantityMetric] DownVotes: Number of downvotes received on user contributions, ranging from 0 to 1,920, average 0.26. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 1,920 with an average of 0.26 across 40,325 entries.
[EntityId] AccountId: Unique system-assigned account identifier, e.g., 4641453, 4605158, 4470101. Contains 40,325 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "4641453", "4605158", "4470101", "1342661", "919636".
[PersonalAttribute] Age: User’s stated age, e.g., 30, 25, 26, with 79% nulls. Contains 70 unique personal attributes, including values like "30", "25", "26", "25", "30", with an average length of 2.0 characters.
[DigitalResource] ProfileImageUrl: URL to the user’s profile image, e.g., https://example.com/avatar.jpg, gravatar.com/..., with 59% nulls. Contains 13,115 unique digital resources across 16,479 entries, with primary domains including , , , ,

== Table Description ==
Nutritional information for 878 recipes, including macronutrients, micronutrients, and caloric breakdowns derived from ingredient compositions.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] recipe_id: Unique identifier for each recipe, such as 1072, 271, or 818. Contains 878 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "1072", "271", "818", "1409", "574".
[QuantityMetric] protein: Protein content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 105.65 g, with typical values like 12.5, 8.3, or 22.1 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 105.65 with an average of 12.20 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] carbo: Carbohydrate content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 116.78 g, with typical values like 25.4, 18.7, or 45.2 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 116.78 with an average of 26.96 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] alcohol: Alcohol content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 14.63 g, with typical values like 0.1, 0.5, or 2.3 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 14.63 with an average of 0.29 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] total_fat: Total fat content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 53.23 g, with typical values like 5.2, 3.8, or 15.6 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 53.23 with an average of 6.58 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] sat_fat: Saturated fat content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 24.49 g, with typical values like 1.2, 0.8, or 4.5 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 24.49 with an average of 1.90 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] cholestrl: Cholesterol content in milligrams per serving, ranging from 0 to 443.04 mg, with typical values like 25.4, 89.1, or 150.3 mg. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 443.04 with an average of 36.17 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] sodium: Sodium content in milligrams per serving, ranging from 0 to 12,092.60 mg, with typical values like 120.5, 450.2, or 890.7 mg. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 12,092.60 with an average of 341.50 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] iron: Iron content in milligrams per serving, ranging from 0 to 30.96 mg, with typical values like 1.5, 2.8, or 5.1 mg. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 30.96 with an average of 2.03 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] vitamin_c: Vitamin C content in milligrams per serving, ranging from 0 to 204.48 mg, with typical values like 15.2, 45.7, or 120.1 mg. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 204.48 with an average of 23.13 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] vitamin_a: Vitamin A content in IU per serving, ranging from 0 to 34,745.57 IU, with typical values like 500, 2,100, or 8,500 IU. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 34,745.57 with an average of 1721.37 across 878 entries.
[QuantityMetric] fiber: Dietary fiber content in grams per serving, ranging from 0 to 8.70 g, with typical values like 0.9, 1.5, or 3.2 g. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 8.70 with an average of 1.12 across 878 entries.
[PercentageMetric] pcnt_cal_carb: Percentage of total calories from carbohydrates, ranging from 0% to 100%, with typical values like 50%, 60%, or 75%. Percentage values ranging from 0 to 100 with an average of 54.87
[PercentageMetric] pcnt_cal_fat: Percentage of total calories from fat, ranging from 0% to 100%, with typical values like 20%, 30%, or 45%. Percentage values ranging from 0 to 100 with an average of 24.13
[PercentageMetric] pcnt_cal_prot: Percentage of total calories from protein, ranging from 0% to 90.24%, with typical values like 15%, 20%, or 30%. Percentage values ranging from 0 to 90.24 with an average of 19.79
[QuantityMetric] calories: Total energy content in calories per serving, ranging from 0 to 892.04 kcal, with typical values like 180, 250, or 420 kcal. Quantitative values ranging from 0 to 892.04 with an average of 217.91 across 878 entries.

== Table Description ==
This table consolidates comprehensive metadata linking donor-funded education projects to the schools where they are implemented, enabling holistic analysis of educational equity, funding patterns, and project characteristics across U.S. schools. It combines school-level contextual data—including geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude), city, state, zip code, district, county, metro area classification, and school type (e.g., charter, magnet, year-round)—with detailed project attributes such as teacher demographics, project focus areas (e.g., math, science, literacy), resource types requested, poverty level indicators (e.g., free/reduced lunch percentage), grade levels served, financial details (funding goal, amount raised), and match eligibility status. With over 664K rows, this table serves as a foundational dataset for understanding how donor resources are distributed across diverse educational contexts, identifying disparities in access to funding, and evaluating the alignment between project needs and donor behavior.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive metadata for over 56,000 Magic: The Gathering cards, integrating core identity, design, pricing, printing, digital integration, and game mechanics attributes across 74 columns. It captures unique card identifiers, artist credits, visual properties (frame, border, color), mana costs, card types, rarity, power/toughness values, and loyalty/life stats. The table includes binary flags for special card states (e.g., reprint, promo, foil, borderless), Commander/Brawl leadership capabilities, and availability indicators. Extensive external identifiers link cards to digital platforms such as MTG Arena, MTGO, Scryfall, TCGplayer, and MCM, enabling cross-system cataloging and tracking. Printing details (set, edition, language) and card text are included alongside sparse but semantically critical fields for variant tracking, collector reference, and digital ecosystem integration. High null rates reflect optional or format-specific attributes, making this a rich, multi-dimensional reference for card analysis, digital marketplaces, and collector databases.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive match-level data for European football (soccer) matches between 2008 and 2016, encompassing 25,979 matches across multiple leagues and countries. It integrates four key dimensions: (1) Match metadata including unique identifiers, league, country, season, stage, date, and final scores for home and away teams; (2) Detailed spatial positioning data for all 22 starting players (11 per team), represented as standardized X and Y coordinates on a pitch grid, where higher X values indicate wider (flank) positions and higher Y values indicate more advanced (attacking) positions; (3) Player-specific event metrics for each starting position, including unique player IDs, goals, shots, fouls, cards, crosses, corners, and possession percentages, encoded as structured XML-like strings capturing event timestamps and types; and (4) Betting odds from 15+ bookmakers (including Bet365, Pinnacle, William Hill, and others), with triplets of home/draw/away odds for each bookmaker, enabling market efficiency analysis and predictive modeling. The table is designed for advanced analytics in sports science, betting strategy, and tactical performance research, with null values in betting columns reflecting missing data from specific bookmakers rather than data quality issues.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive player attribute data for European football players, spanning the years 2007 to 2016, with a total of 183,978 rows and 42 columns. Each row represents a player’s recorded attributes at a specific point in time, identified by a unique player ID and timestamp. The dataset captures both static player traits and dynamic performance metrics, including overall ratings, work rates, and a wide range of technical, physical, and goalkeeping skills. Technical attributes include crossing, dribbling, acceleration, shooting, passing, and defending; physical attributes include agility, balance, strength, and stamina; goalkeeping-specific skills such as diving, handling, and reflexes are also included. All skill ratings are scored on a scale of 1–97, reflecting expert assessments of player capabilities. Missing values are present for certain attributes, particularly among non-goalkeepers for goalkeeping-specific fields, resulting in variable sample sizes per column. This table serves as a rich source for analyzing player performance trends, skill development, and scouting metrics across a decade of European football.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive historical team performance splits for professional hockey teams from 1909 to 2011, capturing win-loss-tie-overtime records across seasons, leagues, and monthly timeframes. It includes team identifiers (tmID), league codes (lgID), and season years (year), with detailed breakdowns of performance at home (h) and on the road (r), as well as monthly statistics from September through April. Each monthly period includes four metrics: Wins (W), Losses (L), Ties (T), and Overtime Losses (OL), reflecting evolving league rules such as the introduction of overtime losses in 1999–2000 and the elimination of ties after 2004–05. The dataset spans multiple leagues including NHL, WHA, and NHA, and is designed to support analysis of team performance trends, home/road differentials, and seasonal momentum — though data sparsity is common in early years and for overtime/tie metrics due to historical rule changes and incomplete reporting.

== Column Information ==
[Year] year: Years spanning 1909 to 2011, with median year 1981 (e.g., 1975, 1990, 2005). Years ranging from 1,909 to 2,011 (median: 1981)
[IndustrySegment] lgID: League identifiers including NHL, WHA, NHA, PCHA, WCHL (e.g., NHL, WHA, NHA). Covers 5 industry segments, including NHL, WHA, NHA, PCHA, WCHL
[EntityId] tmID: Team abbreviations such as CAL, NYI, BOS, BUF, PO1 (e.g., BOS for Boston Bruins, NYI for New York Islanders). Contains 115 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "CAL", "NYI", "PO1", "BOS", "BUF".

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive player-season and postseason statistics for professional basketball players across multiple leagues (including NBA, ABA, NBL, and others) from 1937 to 2011. It includes 23,751 player-season records with 43 columns divided into two main categories: (1) Regular-season performance metrics such as player and team identifiers, season year, stint number, league affiliation, playing time, scoring, rebounding, assists, steals, blocks, turnovers, and shooting efficiency (both cumulative and per-game); and (2) Postseason performance metrics that mirror the regular-season statistics but are specific to playoff games, enabling analysis of player contributions in high-stakes contexts. The data supports longitudinal and comparative analysis of player performance across eras and leagues, integrating both regular and postseason play into a unified record for each player-season.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] id: Unique identifier for each player-team-season record, e.g., '7633', '16029', '15837'. Contains 23,751 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "7633", "16029", "15837", "13766", "7253".

[Year] year: Season year, ranging from 1937 to 2011, with median 1986, e.g., 1985, 1992, 2003. Years ranging from 1,937 to 2,011 (median: 1986)
[Status] stint: Stint number indicating the order of a player’s tenure with a team in a given season, e.g., 1, 2, 0, 3, 4. Status indicators with 6 unique states, most common being 1, 2, 0, 3, 4
[OrganizationUnit] tmID: Team abbreviation code, e.g., 'LAL' (Los Angeles Lakers), 'ATL' (Atlanta Hawks), 'CAR' (Charlotte Hornets). Contains 161 unique organizational units or teams, including "CAR", "ATL", "DTG", "KCK", "LAL".

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive metadata for all California public schools, encompassing administrative, geographic, operational, and contact information. It includes unique school and district identifiers, official school and district names, physical and mailing addresses, phone numbers, operational start and end dates, charter school status, and funding classifications. Additional columns detail school type classifications (DOC, SOC, EdOps, EIL), grade levels offered and served, participation in virtual or magnet programs, and precise geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude). The table also captures up to three hierarchical layers of administrative contact information (names and emails), along with a timestamp indicating the last update. Due to the hierarchical and optional nature of many fields—particularly secondary and tertiary administrative contacts—null values are common in non-primary fields. This dataset serves as the authoritative reference for identifying, locating, and categorizing California public schools within the state’s education system.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive geographic, demographic, economic, and administrative data for U.S. ZIP code areas, integrating foundational and detailed attributes from the 2010 and 2020 U.S. Censuses and other federal sources. It includes core location identifiers (zip_code, city, state), spatial coordinates (latitude, longitude), time zones, and FIPS codes for administrative alignment. The dataset further encompasses population and household statistics, racial and ethnic breakdowns, housing values, income levels, and household composition. Additional columns detail gender-specific population distributions, age groupings, housing unit types, land and water area measurements, mail delivery infrastructure, business activity metrics, payroll data, and counts of Social Security beneficiaries. Core-Based Statistical Area (CBSA) codes are included to enable regional analysis at the metropolitan and micropolitan level. This table serves as a rich resource for geospatial analysis, demographic profiling, urban planning, public policy development, and economic research at the ZIP code level.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive laboratory biomarker data from 13,908 patients, designed for thrombosis risk prediction. It includes 44 biochemical, hematological, immunological, and coagulation parameters collected in clinical practice. The data is organized into two logical groups: (1) Routine metabolic and hematologic markers — including liver function (GOT, GPT, ALP, TP, ALB, T-BIL), kidney function (UN, CRE, UA), lipid profile (T-CHO, TG), muscle damage (CPK), glucose (GLU), and full blood count (WBC, RBC, HGB, HCT, PLT), along with prothrombin time (PT); and (2) Specialized coagulation, immune, and autoimmune markers — including activated partial thromboplastin time (APTT), fibrinogen (FG), plasmin-α2-plasmin inhibitor complex (PIC), thrombin-antithrombin complex (TAT), immunoglobulins (IgG, IgA, IgM), acute-phase reactants (CRP, RF), complement components (C3, C4), and a panel of autoantibodies (RA, RNP, SM, SSA, SSB, CENTROMEA, DNA, DNA-II), as well as urinary protein (U-PRO). The dataset reflects real-world clinical variability with significant missingness, particularly in the specialized serological and autoimmune panels, which are often ordered selectively based on clinical suspicion. Together, these features enable modeling of thrombotic risk through integrated assessment of metabolic dysfunction, inflammation, immune dysregulation, and hypercoagulable states.

== Column Information ==
[EntityId] ID: Unique patient or sample identifier, with examples such as 5302954, 4862013, and 4790235. Contains 302 unique entity identifiers, including examples such as "5302954", "4862013", "4790235", "2409841", "2436816".
[EventTimestamp] Date: Date of laboratory test occurrence, spanning from January 27, 1981 to March 4, 1999. Event timestamps ranging from 1981-01-27T00:00 to 1999-03-04T00:00 across 13,908 recorded occurrences.
[SpecializedMetric] GOT: Aspartate aminotransferase (AST) level; typical values: 20, 35, 150 U/L. Metric values ranging from 3 to 21,480 (mean: 28.80, median: 20.00) across 11,278 records.
[SpecializedMetric] GPT: Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) level; typical values: 18, 45, 200 U/L. Metric values ranging from 1 to 4,780 (mean: 30.45, median: 18.00) across 11,274 records.
[SpecializedMetric] LDH: Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH); typical values: 295, 450, 2000 U/L. Metric values ranging from 25 to 67,080 (mean: 322.27, median: 295) across 11,305 records.
[SpecializedMetric] ALP: Alkaline phosphatase; typical values: 101, 150, 800 U/L. Metric values ranging from 11 to 1,308 (mean: 122.45, median: 101) across 11,151 records.
[SpecializedMetric] TP: Total protein; typical values: 7.1, 6.5, 8.9 g/dL. Metric values ranging from 0 to 9.90 (mean: 7.12, median: 7.10) across 11,118 records.
[SpecializedMetric] ALB: Albumin; typical values: 4.2, 3.8, 5.0 g/dL. Metric values ranging from 1 to 5.80 (mean: 4.14, median: 4.20) across 11,068 records.
[SpecializedMetric] UA: Uric acid; typical values: 4.3, 5.1, 8.5 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 0.40 to 17.30 (mean: 4.40, median: 4.30) across 11,103 records.
[SpecializedMetric] UN: Urea nitrogen; typical values: 14, 20, 50 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 0 to 152 (mean: 15.40, median: 14.00) across 11,238 records.
[SpecializedMetric] CRE: Creatinine; typical values: 0.7, 1.0, 2.5 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 0.10 to 17.10 (mean: 0.79, median: 0.70) across 11,253 records.
[SpecializedMetric] T-BIL: Total bilirubin; typical values: 0.5, 0.8, 2.1 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 0.10 to 7.90 (mean: 0.53, median: 0.50) across 9,621 records.
[SpecializedMetric] T-CHO: Total cholesterol; typical values: 198, 220, 350 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 37 to 568 (mean: 203.06, median: 198.00) across 10,664 records.
[SpecializedMetric] TG: Triglycerides; typical values: 108, 150, 400 mg/dL. Metric values ranging from 1 to 867 (mean: 125.23, median: 108) across 6,437 records.

[SpecializedMetric] RBC: Red blood cell count; typical values: 4.3, 4.8, 5.5 x10⁶/μL. Metric values ranging from 0.40 to 6.60 (mean: 4.32, median: 4.30) across 12,081 records.

[SpecializedMetric] PLT: Platelet count; typical values: 248, 300, 450 x10³/μL. Metric values ranging from 5 to 5,844 (mean: 263.01, median: 248) across 11,287 records.
[SpecializedMetric] PT: Prothrombin time; typical values: 12.4, 14.0, 18.0 seconds. Metric values ranging from 10.10 to 27 (mean: 13.27, median: 12.40) across 621 records.

[Metric] CRP: C-reactive protein; inflammatory marker, reported as qualitative: '+', '<0.3', or '<0.002'. Sample values: "+", "<0.3", "<0.002", "<0.3", "<0.3"
[Metric] RA: Rheumatoid arthritis status; serological result reported as '-', '+', or '2+'. Sample values: "-", "-", "+", "-", "2+"
[Metric] RF: Rheumatoid factor; autoantibody associated with autoimmune disease, commonly 40–148 IU/mL. Metric values ranging from 10 to 4,240 with an average of 148.32 (median: 40.00) across 3,335 records.

== Table Description ==
This table contains comprehensive institutional-level data for 3,798 U.S. colleges and universities, integrating foundational characteristics, financial and demographic indicators, academic performance metrics, and student outcome trajectories. It includes geographic details (city, state), institutional type (level, control, basic classification), enrollment counts, financial aid and endowment figures, and spatial coordinates. Performance metrics span graduation and retention rates for first-time and transfer students over 4- and 6-year periods, faculty full-time status, and outcomes specifically for Pell-eligible students. Many columns include percentile ranks and comparative identifiers for peer institutions. The dataset is designed to support analyses of institutional diversity, funding equity, student mobility, completion disparities, and regional patterns in higher education, with extensive use of null values reflecting incomplete reporting for specific cohorts or years.
